questions.question: What state does a process enter if it is waiting for an I/O operation to finish?
answers: Blocked / Waiting state
Zombie state
Ready state
Running state

questions.question: What is a major disadvantage of the Shortest Job First scheduling algorithm?
answers: Massive memory cache utilization footprints
Long-running processes can suffer from starvation
It requires specialized graphical hardware layers
High context-switching processing overhead

questions.question: What is the main difference between internal and external fragmentation?
answers: Internal occurs inside fixed allocated blocks; external occurs between variable blocks
External happens inside kernel space; internal happens in user space
External happens on hard drives; internal happens inside CPU registers
Internal is fixed by defragmentation; external requires virtual memory flushes

questions.question: What is the function of the Memory Management Unit (MMU)?
answers: To allocate network sockets to background daemons
To clear cache registers when a context switch occurs
To map virtual addresses generated by the CPU to physical memory locations
To partition hard drive sectors dynamically at runtime

questions.question: What is a page fault?
answers: A crash caused by syntax compile errors
A physical hardware failure inside RAM modules
An interrupt triggered when a program accesses a page not currently mapped in RAM
A data anomaly where two files share the same sector

questions.question: What state does a system enter when it spends more time swapping pages than executing instructions?
answers: Thrashing
Paging
Fragmenting
Starvation

questions.question: What does an inode store in a Unix-like file system?
answers: The literal plain-text contents of the target file
File metadata, size, permissions, and block pointers, but not the file name
The human-readable file name and directory path string
The encryption keys used for secure network transmissions

questions.question: What structural pattern does a Hard Link follow compared to a Symbolic Link?
answers: Hard links require administrative privileges to execute
Symbolic links can only reference binary files; hard links handle folders
A Hard Link points directly to the same inode; a Symbolic Link points to a path string
Hard links delete the original file target when broken; symbolic links do not

questions.question: What component maps file paths to matching inode numbers in a file system directory?
answers: File allocation tables (FAT)
Master boot records (MBR)
Virtual file layers (VFS)
Directory entries (dentries)

questions.question: What is the primary role of the Virtual File System (VFS) layer in a kernel?
answers: To provide an abstract uniform interface for applications to access diverse file systems
To simulate internal hard drive arrays inside local ram spaces
To buffer incoming network package flows before delivery to sockets
To manage virtual memory swap spaces on physical storage disks
